message:
fix(fcp-webhook): use abs_diff for timestamp tolerance to avoid i64 overflow

Stripe and Slack webhook timestamp validation used i64 subtraction with
.abs() for the tolerance check. For extreme timestamp values, the i64
subtraction could overflow, and casting a large u64 duration to i64
silently wraps to negative, bypassing the tolerance window.

Switched to i64::abs_diff() which returns u64 and is saturating, then
compares against a u64 tolerance value. Both Stripe and Slack handlers
updated with the same pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
